# GitHub Copilot Instructions

This is a **zero-dependency static website** for LookAtWhatAiCanDo, LLC — an AI consulting company. It uses vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with Firebase Hosting. There is no build step, no package manager, and no framework.

## What This Project Is

- Single-page marketing site (`public/index.html`)
- Dynamic project cards loaded from `public/data/projects.json` at runtime
- `projects.json` is auto-generated by GitHub Actions — never suggest editing it manually

## Stack Rules (Do Not Violate)

- **No npm packages** — do not suggest `npm install`, `package.json` changes, or any third-party libraries
- **No build tools** — no webpack, Vite, Rollup, Parcel, or TypeScript compilation
- **No frameworks** — do not suggest React, Vue, Svelte, Alpine.js, or any other JS framework
- **No CSS frameworks** — do not suggest Tailwind, Bootstrap, or any utility CSS library

## CSS Conventions

Always use the existing CSS custom properties defined in `public/css/style.css`:
- `--black` (#0a0a0a), `--dark` (#111), `--card` (#161616), `--border` (#222)
- `--muted` (#333), `--text` (#e8e8e8), `--dim` (#888), `--accent` (#b8ff57)
- `--red` (#ff4444), `--white` (#ffffff)
- Never hardcode color values; never add inline styles
- Fonts in use: `Bebas Neue` (display), `DM Mono` (body/mono), `Instrument Serif` (italic accent)

## JavaScript Conventions

- Vanilla ES6+ only
- Use `async/await` for all asynchronous operations
- No class-based components or module bundling
- Scripts are loaded via `<script src="js/app.js">` at the bottom of `index.html`

## HTML Conventions

- Semantic HTML5 throughout
- Preserve the existing section structure:
  `nav → hero → services → divider-marquee → projects → philosophy → contact → footer`
- Do not add new `<script>` tags pointing to CDN-hosted libraries

## Deployment

- Push to `main` triggers GitHub Actions: `fetch-projects.js` runs, then Firebase CLI deploys `public/`
- PR pushes deploy to Firebase preview channels
- No manual deployment steps needed

## Local Preview

```bash
open public/index.html
```

No server required — the site works from the filesystem.
